requirements.label: Ship `.env.example` with required variables
requirements.evidence: `4.1 Project Setup`; `5.1 .env.example`

requirements.label: Configure build through env without code edits

requirements.label: Keep secrets out of repo and server-only

requirements.label: Provide app, test, reset command scripts

requirements.label: Include repeatable schema evolution artifacts
requirements.evidence: `2.1 Supabase Schema (Migrations)`; `1.2 Supabase Schema`; `7.6 Data Continuity`

requirements.label: Use one stable namespace per build
requirements.evidence: `2.2 Identity & Isolation` says every query is scoped by env `NAMESPACE_ID`

requirements.label: Isolate namespaces and scope destructive resets
requirements.evidence: `2.1` PK/RLS isolate namespace data; `1.6 Scripts` scopes `npm run test:reset` to the namespace

requirements.source: infra_rider_prd.md > 4.3 Relationship between namespace and user
requirements.evidence: `2.1` primary keys and RLS policies use `(namespace_id, user_id)`

requirements.label: Support documented dev auth injection, prod-gated

requirements.source: infra_rider_prd.md > 5.2 Migration to real OAuth must be straightforward
requirements.evidence: `2.2 Identity & Isolation` says real OAuth swaps in with "no schema change needed"

requirements.label: Keep backend as persisted source of truth
requirements.evidence: `1.3 Key Architectural Principles` says backend is source of truth; `9 Key Business Rules` repeats it

requirements.label: Make client cache safe to discard
requirements.evidence: `1.3 Key Architectural Principles`; `9 Key Business Rules` says clearing client storage must not lose user data

requirements.label: Avoid Docker requirement for cloud-agent compatibility
requirements.evidence: `1.1 Tech Stack` says Supabase can be hosted or local
requirements.gap: The plan implies a hosted path but never explicitly documents Docker as optional or unnecessary for benchmark execution.

requirements.label: Overlay saved user data on every show appearance
requirements.evidence: `1.5 useShow` merges Supabase and TMDB data; `7.1 Show Tile Indicators`; `9 Key Business Rules` says the user's version takes precedence everywhere
requirements.gap: The plan states the rule, but it only concretely specifies badges and merged detail fetches rather than a shared overlay mechanism for every show surface.

requirements.label: Support visible statuses plus hidden `Next`
requirements.evidence: `2.1` `my_status` enum includes `next`; `4.4 My Relationship` omits it from visible chips
requirements.gap: The plan preserves `Next` in the schema but never defines its hidden, non-primary-UI treatment.

requirements.label: Keep one consistent AI persona across surfaces

requirements.gap: The plan does not establish a single shared persona contract or reusable base prompt to keep all surfaces aligned.

requirements.label: Enforce shared AI guardrails across all surfaces
requirements.evidence: `3.2` prompt descriptions and `9 Key Business Rules Checklist` mention domain and spoiler guardrails
requirements.gap: The guardrails appear as scattered prompt notes rather than a shared cross-surface enforcement contract.

requirements.label: Make AI warm, joyful, and light in critique
requirements.evidence: `3.2 Scoop Prompt` is warm and opinionated; `3.2 Ask Prompt` is conversational
requirements.gap: The plan captures warmth and opinionation but does not explicitly preserve the joy-forward, gentle-critique requirement across surfaces.

requirements.label: Structure Scoop as personal taste mini-review
requirements.evidence: `3.2 Scoop Prompt` defines a structured mini blog post of taste

requirements.label: Keep Ask brisk and dialogue-like by default
requirements.evidence: `3.2 Ask Prompt` uses a conversational-friend persona rather than an essay format

requirements.label: Feed AI the right surface-specific context inputs
requirements.evidence: `3.2` lists surface-specific inputs for Scoop, Ask, Concepts, Alchemy, and summarization

requirements.label: Validate discovery with rubric and hard-fail integrity
requirements.evidence: none
requirements.gap: The plan never introduces a discovery-quality rubric or a hard-fail validation gate for real-show integrity and recommendation quality.

top_gaps.label: Enforce shared AI guardrails across all surfaces
top_gaps.why: Without a shared guardrail contract, AI behavior can drift by surface on spoiler safety, domain limits, and honesty, creating inconsistent product behavior even if each prompt looks reasonable in isolation.

top_gaps.label: Overlay saved user data on every show appearance
top_gaps.why: If saved overlays do not follow a show everywhere it appears, the product breaks its central promise that the library is the user's personalized lens on the catalog.

top_gaps.label: Resolve AI recommendations to real selectable shows
top_gaps.why: If AI recommendations are not validated and resolved consistently across Ask, Explore Similar, and Alchemy, users will hit dead-end or untrustworthy suggestions on the app's core discovery paths.

top_gaps.label: Re-add preserves My Data and refreshes public data
top_gaps.why: If removed shows lose their prior relationship data permanently, users cannot trust that the app remembers their history when they revisit a title later.

top_gaps.label: Validate discovery with rubric and hard-fail integrity
top_gaps.why: Without a discovery-quality rubric and integrity gate, the app can ship hallucinated, off-brand, or weakly grounded recommendations without any explicit plan to catch them.

narrative.overall_posture: This is a strong plan with concentrated but meaningful gaps rather than a structurally weak one. The architecture, persistence model, primary navigation, core collection flows, and major screen breakdowns are all concrete, but several critical semantics remain underspecified around AI behavior contracts, actionability of AI outputs, and preservation of user-specific data.
narrative.strength_clusters: The plan is strongest in Benchmark Runtime & Isolation, App Navigation & Discover Shell, Show Detail & Relationship UX mechanics, Person Detail, and Settings & Export. It also covers most of Collection Data & Persistence well at the default/save/remove rule level, especially timestamps in the schema, export, and namespace-plus-user scoping.
narrative.weakness_clusters: The gaps cluster around AI Voice, Persona & Quality, then secondarily around Collection Data & Persistence and Concepts, Explore Similar & Alchemy. The pattern is not random: the plan is weaker wherever the product depends on strict cross-surface behavioral contracts, especially for AI guardrails, recommendation resolution, overlay fidelity, and re-add semantics.
narrative.risk_assessment: If this plan were executed as-is, the app would likely function and cover the major journeys, but QA and stakeholders would first notice inconsistency in AI behavior and actionability. The most visible failure modes are AI suggestions that are not consistently validated into real shows, overlay behavior that is thinner than promised outside detail views, and uncertainty about whether user history survives a remove-and-revisit cycle.
narrative.remediation_guidance: The remaining planning work is mostly about tightening contracts, not redesigning the app. Add a shared AI behavior spec that all surfaces inherit, define a discovery-quality validation pass with integrity hard-fails, make recommendation-resolution behavior explicit for every AI surface, and specify how removed shows preserve recoverable user history alongside the persistence boundaries for sync and transient TMDB detail data.
